title: Metalasia hispida
taxonomy: Metalasia hispida is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Metalasia. This plant is native to South Africa. Metalasia hispida is further classified as a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae. It is a flowering plant with unique characteristics.
uses: Metalasia hispida is traditionally used in South African folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are sometimes employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's antimicrobial properties make it useful for treating wounds and skin infections. In traditional medicine, Metalasia hispida is also used to manage respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and benefits.

intro: Metalasia hispida, commonly known as the hairy metalasia or hairy metalis, is a shrub-like plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Metalasia hispida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Metalasia hispida is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, and is also used in traditional South African medicine to make remedies for various health conditions.
